_meta.unit: Other-houses queue, house 11 (LAST) - Loewe lexicon: models (with debut year + era) + materials/signature-motifs (the 1970 Vicente Vela Anagram + Spanish leather craft) + named colors. The 'other houses' first pass is now COMPLETE.

types.model: bag model; the house-assigned name is the canonical key (Regime A). KEY FACT: Jonathan Anderson (JW Anderson) was CD 2013-March 2025 and created essentially the entire modern bag canon (Puzzle 2015, Hammock re-intro 2016, Basket 2017, Squeeze 2023, Pebble Bucket 2024, Puzzle Fold 2023, plus the viral Pigeon/Tomato clutches). He LEFT for Dior in 2025; Proenza Schouler's Jack McCollough + Lazaro Hernandez are the new co-CDs, debut spring 2026 (Loewe's 180th anniv, the Amazona 180).
types.material: signature motif / leather / craft technique / logo. The house DNA is Spanish leather craft (handmade in Madrid since 1846, oldest LVMH house, acquired 1996) + the Anagram (four interlocking/scrolling L's, designed 1970 by Spanish artist Vicente Vela). Craft vocabulary: patchwork leather (the Puzzle), handwoven raffia/palm-leaf (the Basket), leather marquetry, nappa calfskin/lambskin/goatskin, the repeat-Anagram jacquard.
types.color: named color / seasonal colorway. KEY HONEST FINDING (a MIDDLE case): Loewe DOES publish a per-season color list (loewe.com SS24 Flamenco: Emerald Green, Dark Burgundy, Sahara, Black), which is MORE than Dior/Gucci/YSL/Celine/Fendi/Prada do, but the names are MOSTLY DESCRIPTIVE (Emerald Green, Black) with the occasional evocative one (Sahara), so it is LESS than the deep Hermes/Bottega proprietary lexicon. Loewe sits BETWEEN the two camps. official_or_descriptive marks each.

_meta.caveat: Regime A house. ANDERSON ERA (2013-2025) defines the modern canon - attribute bags debuting 2014-2025 to him; McCollough/Hernandez are the new co-CDs (spring 2026 debut, the Amazona 180). House DNA = Spanish leather craft (Madrid, 1846) + the Anagram (1970, Vicente Vela). COLORS: Loewe is the MIDDLE case - it labels seasonal colorways (sourced: SS24 Flamenco four colors) but mostly descriptively, NOT a deep proprietary lexicon. NOT invented. HELD RIGOR: 'Cushion/Puffer Goya', a 2022 'Pebble' distinct from the SS2024 Pebble Bucket, and the 'Anton' men's bag did NOT surface - logged as leads, NOT invented or dated (the Goya + Pebble Bucket ARE sourced). Squeeze resolved to 2023 (brief said 2024); Hammock resolved to spring 2016 re-intro (brief said 2018). CONFLICTS flagged not smoothed: Flamenco origin 1970s (Vogue) vs 1980s (ottodisanpietro); Puzzle leather-piece count ~9 (Vogue) vs 75 (Fashionphile, likely panels-vs-total). loewe.com NOT scraped wholesale (Akamai bot-shell risk per LV/Dior/Gucci/BV/YSL/Celine/Fendi/Prada); the SS24 Flamenco color list + Anagram-2025-half came via loewe.com/official-FB SEARCH SNIPPETS. Christie's Loewe artist page scraped clean (auction-grade: Anagram 1970, founded 1846, Anderson 2013, Puzzle 2015 to Squeeze 2023, Gate tote 2019, a 'Barcelona'-style heritage bag). Sources: Vogue 'Best Loewe Handbags of 2026' (the WORKHORSE, per-model debut years), Fashionphile 'Anderson Retrospective' (the era + full-canon WORKHORSE), Christie's Loewe artist page (auction-grade), loewe.com SS24 Flamenco (the color source), official Loewe FB (Anagram 1970 + leather marquetry).
conflicts: Hammock debut: brief says 2018; Vogue says Anderson re-introduced it spring 2016 (after a 1999 archive style) - logged spring 2016 high
Squeeze debut: brief says 2024; Vogue + Christie's say fall 2023 - logged 2023 high (2024 = a beaded FW2024 variant)
Flamenco origin decade: Vogue says launched 1970s; ottodisanpietro says first introduced in the 1980s - logged 1970s high, conflict flagged not smoothed
Puzzle leather-piece count: Vogue ~9 pieces precisely patchworked vs Fashionphile 75 pieces - both logged, likely major-panels vs total-cut-pieces, flagged not smoothed
Gate debut year: not cleanly sourced (~2017 beat); a Gate tote auctioned dated 2019 (Christie's) - logged medium
sources: facebook.com/LOEWE (Anagram adapted from original 1970 Vicente Vela work; Gate crossbody; leather marquetry)

coverage.models: 19 model rows (Puzzle 2015, Hammock 2016, Gate ~2017/auction-2019, Flamenco 1970s, Amazona 1975, Amazona 180 SS2026, Goya, Basket 2017, Squeeze 2023, Puzzle Fold 2023, Pebble Bucket SS2024, Balloon, Paseo, Anagram Tote, Cubi, Luna, Barcelona heritage, Pigeon Clutch, Tomato Clutch 2024) + 3 honest leads (Cushion/Puffer Goya, a 2022 'Pebble', Anton men's) logged not invented
coverage.materials: 7 motifs/materials (the Anagram 1970 Vicente Vela + Spanish leather craft 1846/Craft Prize 2016 + patchwork leather + handwoven raffia + leather marquetry + nappa/lambskin/goatskin leathers + repeat-Anagram jacquard)
coverage.colors: 4 house-published SS24 Flamenco colors (Emerald Green/Dark Burgundy/Sahara/Black, sourced loewe.com) + 3 editorial/reseller descriptors (pistachio-oxblood, bold-red/striking-azure, Natural Tan) + the explicit MIDDLE-CASE finding. NO invented seasonal color names.

name: Puzzle
note: Anderson's FIRST bag and the modern Loewe icon: an origami/geometric box patchworked from leather panels, folds flat; 'a deconstruction of a traditional bag into a three-dimensional object with flat functionality'; Loewe's first new shape since the 1980s. First seen at the men's spring 2015 show; handcrafted in Madrid. Triple-sourced: Vogue (men's spring 2015) + Fashionphile (Anderson's first big statement, origami, first new shape since the 1980s) + PurseBlog (Anderson's first bag). Leather-piece-count conflict flagged: Vogue ~9 vs Fashionphile 75.

name: Hammock
note: Shape-shifting shoulder bag with supple flexible sides that fold origami-like into different shapes; the Hammock Flip adds buckles+zips to morph top-handle to hobo. Re-introduced by Anderson SPRING 2016 after a 1999 archive style. Brief said 2018 - resolved to spring 2016 per Vogue.

name: Gate
note: Contemporary saddle-aesthetic crossbody/tote: rounded flap, knotted leather strap detail, equestrian/saddle references. Anderson era (~2017 beat); a small Gate tote auctioned dated 2019 (Christie's). Exact debut year not cleanly sourced this run - logged medium, NOT fabricated.

name: Flamenco
note: Heritage drawstring pouch/clutch: soft unadorned nappa-calfskin body, knotted drawstring pulls, a 'blank canvas' for color. Launched 1970s; reprised 2010 by Stuart Vevers (tassels); reworked by Anderson (knots + embossed Anagram); McCollough/Hernandez added metal coils on the pull-knots for spring 2026. Origin-decade conflict flagged: Vogue 1970s vs ottodisanpietro 1980s - logged 1970s, not smoothed.

name: Amazona
note: The heritage doctor's-bag tote: rectangular frame, two top handles, embossed Loewe logo + functioning mini-padlock; envisioned for the 1970s female workforce. Debuted 1975; reintroduced by Anderson fall 2021 (pegged to Loewe's 175th anniversary). Sizes = bag width in cm. All-leather or Anagram canvas body.

name: Amazona 180
note: The spring-2026 re-cut of the Amazona: single top handle, two long leather zip pullers, designed to be left open, updated logo peeking out, suede-lined. Marks Loewe's 180th anniversary AND the McCollough/Hernandez debut show (their first signature move after Anderson's departure).

name: Goya
note: Minimalist structured flap/top-handle bag named for the Spanish painter Goya; clean lines, restrained hardware; an Anderson-era 'quiet' shape. Identity sourced (Fashionphile: Anderson created the minimalist Goya); debut year not cleanly sourced - logged medium, NOT fabricated. The brief's 'Cushion/Puffer Goya' variant is a lead, not separately sourced.

name: Basket
note: The handwoven palm-leaf/raffia market-basket tote with fine-leather trim; handwoven in Spain; Anderson's 'craft-is-cool' elevation of the humble market bag. Debuted at the spring 2017 womenswear show. Year-round It bag; many colors+sizes. The Paula's Ibiza woven line extends this craft.

name: Squeeze
note: Tactile squishy lambskin shoulder bag with a plush squeezable handle + chain strap; moody-meets-powdery hues (pistachios, oxblood). Introduced at Loewe's FALL 2023 runway. FW2024 versions added tapestry-weave beadwork (jungly flora/fauna). Brief said 2024 - resolved to 2023 (Vogue + Christie's both date it 2023; 2024 = the beaded variant).

name: Puzzle Fold
note: An origami tote (Puzzle-family extension) that pops open then collapses flat into a neat square; calfskin; mini/medium/XL; the viral summer-2023 unboxing bag. Launched July 2023. Double-sourced: Vogue (folds flat, July 2023) + Fashionphile (the Puzzle Fold Tote, a Puzzle-family bag).

name: Pebble Bucket
note: Structured-meets-slouchy bucket bag named for its only hardware, a bulbous Anagram-branded 'pebble' medallion; adjustable belt-hole strap; gold or silver pebble; three sizes. Debuted at the men's SPRING/SUMMER 2024 runway; one of Anderson's final creations. NOTE: the brief's 'Pebble (2022)' as a separate bag is unresolved - a 2022 'Pebble' distinct from this SS2024 Pebble Bucket was NOT sourced (logged as a lead, 2022 NOT asserted).

name: Balloon
note: A redefined bucket-shape bag (a rounded, balloon-like bucket). Identity sourced (Fashionphile: the Balloon bag redefined bucket shapes); debut year not cleanly sourced - logged medium. Also an Anderson 'Balloon heels' footwear motif (deflated-balloon heels).

name: Paseo
note: A relaxed, soft shoulder bag (an Anderson 'evolution' shape); shiny nappa calfskin, often with a chain. Identity sourced (Fashionphile: the relaxed Paseo bag); debut year not cleanly sourced - logged medium, NOT fabricated.

name: Anagram Tote
note: A tote that 'boldly carries the brand's identity' (the Anagram front and center). Identity sourced (Fashionphile); debut year not cleanly sourced - logged medium.

name: Cubi
note: An understated boxy/structured bag (Anderson era), seen in jacquard Anagram calfskin (e.g. Avocado Green). Identity sourced (Fashionphile: Anderson launched the understated Cubi); debut year not cleanly sourced - logged medium.

name: Luna
note: A half-moon shoulder bag. Identity sourced (Fashionphile: the half-moon Luna bag, seen in satin calfskin jacquard); debut year not cleanly sourced - logged medium.

name: Barcelona
note: A heritage Loewe handbag shape (pre-Anderson); appears in an auction lot as a slate-blue leather ''Barcelona'-style' Loewe handbag. Confirms 'Barcelona' is a real Loewe model name; debut year + full identity not sourced this run - logged low, NOT fabricated.

name: Pigeon Clutch
note: The viral hyper-realistic pigeon-shaped clutch (feathered wings, tiny claws); carried by Sarah Jessica Parker in 'And Just Like That'; art-as-accessory. Part of Anderson's heritage-animal bag collection (also elephant, bunny with knot-tie ears, frog clutches). Debut year not cleanly sourced - logged medium.

name: Tomato Clutch
note: The 'meme to reality' tomato-shaped clutch: in 2024 @homocowboi tweeted an heirloom tomato ('this tomato is so Loewe'), Anderson reposted it and unveiled the Tomato Clutch days later captioned 'Loewe meme to reality.' The internet-bag engagement archetype.

name: Anagram (four interlocking L's)
note: THE Loewe signature mark: a logo of four scrolling/interlocking L's, designed in 1970 by Spanish artist Vicente Vela. Embossed, used as hardware (the Pebble medallion), and as a repeat jacquard. In 2025 the house began also using a single half of the Anagram as a mark. Triple-sourced: Vogue + Christie's (designed 1970 by Vicente Vela) + official Loewe FB (adapted from the original 1970 Vicente Vela work). THE GEO-valuable decoder.

name: Spanish leather craft (handmade in Madrid)
note: The house DNA: Loewe began as a Madrid leather-artisan collective in 1846 (oldest LVMH house; LVMH acquired it 1996; official supplier to the Spanish Royal Crown 1905 under King Alfonso XIII) and still hand-makes its bags in Madrid. Anderson's whole platform was 'craft is cool' (Loewe Craft Prize founded 2016). Sourced: Vogue (founded 1846, Madrid's Lobo Street) + Fashionphile (collective 1846, LVMH 1996) + Christie's (Royal Crown 1905, Craft Prize 2016).

name: Patchwork leather (the Puzzle construction)
note: The Puzzle's signature build: precise patchwork of leather panels into an origami box that folds flat; the process takes ~nine hours. Piece-count conflict flagged: Vogue 'approximately nine pieces of leather precisely patchworked' vs Fashionphile '75 pieces of leather' - likely major panels vs total cut pieces, both logged, not smoothed.

name: Handwoven raffia / palm-leaf weave
note: Loewe's woven craft: handwoven palm-leaf/raffia/iraca-palm baskets with fine-leather trim, woven in Spain (the Basket bag + the Paula's Ibiza line). Sourced: Vogue (the Basket, handwoven in Spain, leather trim) + Fashionphile (Iraca Palm Anagram Basket, Raffia Basket Tote).

name: Leather marquetry
note: A Loewe craft technique: inlaying differently-colored leathers like wood marquetry (used on the T Pouch Cat and other pieces). Sourced: official Loewe FB (the T Pouch Cat features the house's Leather Marquetry technique).

name: Nappa calfskin / lambskin / pebbled calfskin / goatskin
note: The Loewe leather family: the Puzzle runs in calfskin, pebbled calfskin, and goatskin; the Flamenco in nappa calfskin; the Squeeze in supple lambskin. Sourced: Vogue per-model material notes.

name: Repeat Anagram (jacquard / canvas)
note: The Anagram used as an all-over repeat pattern on canvas/jacquard (e.g. the jacquard Anagram Cubi; repeat-Anagram canvas vintage pieces). Sourced: Fashionphile (Jacquard Anagram Calfskin Cubi) + kewaybags (the repeated Anagram pattern, originally created in the 1970s by Vicente Vela, on canvas with leather trim).

name: Emerald Green
note: One of the four house-published SS24 Flamenco Purse colours (Emerald Green, Dark Burgundy, Sahara, Black). House-published per-season name but literal/descriptive. The MIDDLE-CASE evidence: Loewe DOES label seasonal colorways (more than Dior/Gucci/etc.) but mostly descriptively (less than Hermes/Bottega).

name: Dark Burgundy
note: One of the four house-published SS24 Flamenco Purse colours. Literal/descriptive house-published name.

name: Sahara
note: One of the four house-published SS24 Flamenco Purse colours; the one evocative/proprietary-leaning name (a desert-sand tone) - the closest Loewe gets to a poetic color name in this list. The needle that shows Loewe is the MIDDLE case, not the bare-descriptor camp.

name: Black
note: One of the four house-published SS24 Flamenco Purse colours. Literal/descriptive house-published name.

name: Pistachio / oxblood
note: The Squeeze's 'moody-meets-powdery' palette in editorial descriptors (pistachios, oxblood). NOT house-published color names - logged as descriptors at medium, not promoted to official.

name: Bold red / striking azure
note: The current Flamenco 'vibrant brights' in editorial descriptors (a bold red to striking azure). NOT house-published color names - logged as descriptors.

name: Natural Tan
note: The Basket/raffia natural color (a reseller/product descriptor; e.g. Raffia Basket Tote Natural Tan, Iraca Palm Anagram Basket Natural Tan). A descriptive shop name, not a proprietary house color.
